meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Fix Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug your AI app? Learn to validate your idea before coding to prevent technical debt. A student-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting syntax errors in poorly scoped projects. By validating your core idea first, you ensure technical feasibility before writing a single line of code. Use Antigravity to architect your logic and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, turning complex debugging nightmares into manageable, modular, and high-quality development sprints.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours staring at a terminal glowing with cryptic red error messages. The initial excitement of your AI project has curdled into a frustrating cycle of endless debugging, where fixing one bug triggers three more. You feel like you're drowning in code that you don't fully understand, and the finish line seems to retreat further with every commit.

This isn't a failure of intelligence; it's a failure of scope. Most student projects collapse because the architecture was never stress-tested against the reality of the implementation. By shifting your focus from 'coding blindly' to 'validated engineering,' you regain control. We are going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to transform your workflow, ensuring your project is built on a foundation that actually works.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Value Proposition
steps.description: Before touching the IDE, isolate the one core feature that provides value. If you can't describe the logic in three sentences, your code will be too complex to debug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Analyze my project idea: [INSERT IDEA]. Breakdown the core logic into an atomic, testable flow chart. Identify potential technical bottlenecks that would make debugging impossible.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this project scope and identify the most likely point of failure for an intermediate developer.'

steps.title: Architect for Observability
steps.description: Don't just write code; write code that tells you why it's failing. Build your project with logging and error-handling as first-class citizens.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust error-handling scaffold for a Python/TS AI application. Include custom exceptions and structured logging patterns that make debugging trivial.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement structured logging across the main service modules to capture state transitions.'

steps.title: Validate Logic with Scaffold Testing
steps.description: Create a 'skeleton' version of your app. This confirms the logic works before you wrap it in complex UI or third-party integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a set of unit tests for my core logic: [INSERT LOGIC]. Focus on edge cases where the AI output might hallucinate or fail.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the unit tests for the core logic layer and report any failures.'

steps.title: Automated Debugging Loops
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform an 'autopsy' on your code. Feed the error logs back into the model to identify the root cause systematically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to interpret stack traces in my specific stack. Provide a checklist for systematic bug isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude debug 'Analyze the last 50 lines of logs and suggest a refactor to prevent the current recursion error.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement
steps.description: Once the core is stable, expand slowly. Never add a new feature until the existing code is bug-free and documented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase. Identify technical debt that will hinder future feature development and suggest a cleanup plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Clean up the codebase by modularizing the API calls into separate, testable functions.'

pro_tips: Treat every error message as a specific data point, not a personal insult to your coding ability.
If you cannot explain a block of code to a rubber duck in 30 seconds, delete it and simplify the logic.
Use Claude Code to generate 'docstrings' for every function; the process of documenting often reveals the bug.
Always build the 'Happy Path' first, then systematically add error states one by one.
If you find yourself stuck for more than 30 minutes, switch context or commit your progress and walk away; your brain needs the reset.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, deploy your project to a staging environment (like Vercel or Railway) to see how it handles real-world traffic, then begin your first feature expansion.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the methodology to stop guessing and start building. Don't let a few bugs stall your career momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Before touching the IDE, isolate the one core feature that provides value. If you can't describe the logic in three sentences, your code will be too complex to debug.
Don't just write code; write code that tells you why it's failing. Build your project with logging and error-handling as first-class citizens.
Create a 'skeleton' version of your app. This confirms the logic works before you wrap it in complex UI or third-party integrations.
Use Claude Code to perform an 'autopsy' on your code. Feed the error logs back into the model to identify the root cause systematically.
Once the core is stable, expand slowly. Never add a new feature until the existing code is bug-free and documented.
